# GitHub Copilot Instructions for MicroservicesArchitecture-Web

This Angular project follows strict coding standards and best practices for Angular 20+ development.

## 📖 IMPORTANT: Read Documentation First

**BEFORE writing any code, ALWAYS read these documentation files:**

1. **`docs/PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md`** - Understand the project architecture, folder structure, and detailed patterns
2. **`docs/QUICK_START.md`** - Review common patterns, code examples, and best practices
3. **`docs/SETUP_COMPLETE.md`** - Understand what's already set up and available features
4. **`docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md`** - Common errors and solutions (READ THIS WHEN ENCOUNTERING ERRORS)

These files contain essential information about:

- Project structure and where to place different types of code
- Established patterns and conventions already in use
- Available services, components, and utilities
- Theme system, routing patterns, and testing approaches
- Common errors and how to fix them (PowerShell paths, Angular animations, Material theming)

**Do not create new patterns or structures without first checking if they already exist in the documentation.**

## 🚨 CRITICAL: Common Errors to Avoid

### 1. PowerShell Terminal Commands

**ALWAYS use quotes for paths with spaces in PowerShell:**

```powershell
# ✅ CORRECT
cd "C:\Users\Hady Joukhadar\Desktop\Projects\..." ; npx nx serve playground

# ❌ WRONG - Will fail with paths containing spaces
cd C:\Users\Hady Joukhadar\Desktop\Projects\...
```

**Use semicolon (`;`) not `&&` to chain commands in PowerShell.**

### 2. Angular Material 20+ Theming

**Angular Material 20+ uses M3 theming API, NOT the old M2 API:**

```scss
// ✅ CORRECT for Angular Material 20+
$theme: mat.define-theme(
  (
    color: (
      theme-type: light,
      primary: mat.$violet-palette,
    ),
  )
);

// ❌ WRONG - Old M2 API (will cause "Undefined function" error)
$primary: mat.define-palette(mat.$indigo-palette);
$theme: mat.define-light-theme(...);
```

### 3. Angular Animations Dependency

**Angular Material requires `@angular/animations` package:**

```bash
# Install matching version
npm install @angular/animations@~20.3.0
```

**If you see error:** `Cannot find module '@angular/animations/browser'`
**Solution:** Install the animations package (see TROUBLESHOOTING.md)

### 4. Project Paths in project.json

**All paths in `project.json` must be relative to workspace root, even though the file is inside the app folder:**

```json
// ✅ CORRECT - paths from workspace root
{
  "build": {
    "options": {
      "tsConfig": "apps/playground/tsconfig.app.json",
      "browser": "apps/playground/src/main.ts"
    }
  }
}

// ❌ WRONG - relative paths from project folder
{
  "build": {
    "options": {
      "tsConfig": "tsconfig.app.json",
      "browser": "src/main.ts"
    }
  }
}
```

## 🎯 Core Principle: MINIMAL CODE

Write the **MINIMUM** amount of code necessary. No over-engineering, no unnecessary abstractions, no future-proofing.

## 📁 Component Organization (MANDATORY)

**All files related to a component MUST be colocated in the same folder:**

```
user/
├── user.component.ts           # Component
├── user.component.html         # Template
├── user.component.scss         # Styles
├── user.service.ts             # Service
├── user.model.ts               # Entity (Interface + Class)
├── user.resolver.ts            # Resolver (if needed)
├── user.guard.ts               # Guard (if needed)
└── user.types.ts               # Type definitions (if needed)
```

**Example:**

```typescript
// user/user.model.ts
export interface IUser {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
}

export class User implements IUser {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;

  constructor(data: Partial<IUser> = {}) {
    this.id = data.id || '';
    this.name = data.name || '';
    this.email = data.email || '';
  }
}

// user/user.service.ts
@Injectable({ providedIn: 'root' })
export class UserService {
  private _http = inject(HttpClient);

  getUser(id: string) {
    return this._http.get<User>(`/api/users/${id}`);
  }
}

// user/user.component.ts
@Component({
  selector: 'app-user',
  standalone: true,
  templateUrl: './user.component.html',
  styleUrl: './user.component.scss',
})
export class UserComponent {
  private _userService = inject(UserService);
  userId = input.required<string>();
  user = signal<User | null>(null);
}
```

**Key Rules:**

- ✅ Component and its service in the same folder
- ✅ All related files (models, resolvers, guards, types) colocated
- ✅ No separate `/services`, `/models`, `/resolvers` folders per component
- ❌ Don't scatter component files across different directories
- ❌ Don't create deep nested folder structures

**Exception:** Shared services, models, and utilities that are used by multiple components should be in `libs/core` or `libs/shared`.

## 📝 Mandatory Naming Conventions

### Variables

- **Private variables**: `_variableName` (underscore prefix)
- **Public variables**: `variableName` (no prefix)
- **Observables**: `variable$` (dollar suffix)
- **Signals**: `variableName` (no special prefix/suffix)

```typescript
export class UserComponent {
  // Private
  private _userId = signal<string>('');
  private _userService = inject(UserService);

  // Public
  userName = signal<string>('');
  isLoading = signal<boolean>(false);

  // Observables
  users$ = this._userService.getUsers();

  // Signal inputs/outputs
  userId = input.required<string>();
  userChanged = output<User>();
}
```

### Entities (Models)

- **ALWAYS** create both `Interface` and `Class` for each entity
- Place in `core/models/` or `feature/models/`

```typescript
// Interface for type checking
export interface IUser {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
}

// Class for entity with methods and defaults
export class User implements IUser {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;

  constructor(data: Partial<IUser> = {}) {
    this.id = data.id || '';
    this.name = data.name || '';
    this.email = data.email || '';
  }

  get displayName(): string {
    return this.name;
  }
}
```

## 🎨 Component Patterns

### Signal-Based Inputs/Outputs (MANDATORY)

**NEVER** use `@Input()` or `@Output()` decorators.

```typescript
// ❌ DON'T USE
@Input() userId!: string;
@Output() userChanged = new EventEmitter<User>();

// ✅ ALWAYS USE
userId = input.required<string>();
showAvatar = input<boolean>(true);
userClicked = output<User>();
```

### Dependency Injection

Use `inject()` function instead of constructor injection:

```typescript
export class UserService {
  private _http = inject(HttpClient);

  getUsers() {
    return this._http.get<User[]>('/api/users');
  }
}
```

### Reactive Forms (MANDATORY)

**ALWAYS use Reactive Forms with Typed Forms. Template-driven forms are NOT allowed.**

```typescript
import { Component, inject } from '@angular/core';
import {
  FormBuilder,
  FormGroup,
  ReactiveFormsModule,
  Validators,
} from '@angular/forms';
import { MatFormFieldModule } from '@angular/material/form-field';
import { MatInputModule } from '@angular/material/input';
import { MatButtonModule } from '@angular/material/button';

// ✅ Define interface for form model
interface IUserForm {
  name: string;
  email: string;
  age: number | null;
}

export class UserFormComponent {
  private _fb = inject(FormBuilder);

  // ✅ Create TYPED FormGroup with FormBuilder
  userForm: FormGroup<IUserForm> = this._fb.group<IUserForm>({
    name: ['', [Validators.required, Validators.minLength(3)]],
    email: ['', [Validators.required, Validators.email]],
    age: [null, [Validators.min(18)]],
  });

  onSubmit(): void {
    if (this.userForm.valid) {
      const formValue: IUserForm = this.userForm.value; // Type-safe!
      console.log(formValue);
    }
  }
}
```

**Template:**

```html
<form [formGroup]="userForm" (ngSubmit)="onSubmit()">
  <mat-form-field>
    <mat-label>Name</mat-label>
    <input matInput formControlName="name" />
    @if (userForm.get('name')?.hasError('required')) {
    <mat-error>Name is required</mat-error>
    }
  </mat-form-field>

  <mat-form-field>
    <mat-label>Email</mat-label>
    <input matInput type="email" formControlName="email" />
    @if (userForm.get('email')?.hasError('email')) {
    <mat-error>Invalid email</mat-error>
    }
  </mat-form-field>

  <button
    mat-raised-button
    color="primary"
    type="submit"
    [disabled]="userForm.invalid"
  >
    Submit
  </button>
</form>
```

**Key Points:**

- ✅ Use `FormBuilder`, `FormGroup`, `FormControl`
- ✅ **ALWAYS use Typed Forms** (`FormGroup<IUserForm>`)
- ✅ Define interface for form model
- ✅ Use `inject(FormBuilder)` for DI
- ✅ Import `ReactiveFormsModule` in component
- ✅ Use `[formGroup]` and `formControlName` in template
- ✅ Use Angular Material form components (`mat-form-field`, `mat-input`, etc.)
- ❌ NEVER use `[(ngModel)]` or template-driven forms
- ❌ NEVER use untyped `FormGroup` without generic type

## 🎨 Styling Standards

### CSS Variables for Theming

- Use CSS variables for ALL colors and theme values
- Support dark/light modes
- Support RTL/LTR directions

```scss
:root {
  --primary-color: #3f51b5;
  --background-color: #ffffff;
  --text-color: #000000;
  --spacing-md: 16px;
  --border-radius-md: 8px;
}

[data-theme='dark'] {
  --background-color: #121212;
  --text-color: #ffffff;
}
```

### SCSS Nesting (MANDATORY)

- **ALWAYS** nest styles
- **ALWAYS** start from `:host`
- **NEVER** wrap content in unnecessary divs
- Style the component host element directly

```scss
// ✅ CORRECT
:host {
  display: flex;
  gap: var(--spacing-md);

  img {
    width: 100px;
    border-radius: var(--border-radius-md);

    &:hover {
      transform: scale(1.05);
    }
  }

  // RTL Support
  :host-context([dir='rtl']) & {
    flex-direction: row-reverse;
  }
}

// ❌ WRONG - No wrapper divs
main {
  display: flex;
}
```

## 📦 Angular Material

Use Angular Material for all UI components. Import only needed modules.

```typescript
import { MatCardModule } from "@angular/material/card";
import { MatButtonModule } from "@angular/material/button";
import { MatIconModule } from "@angular/material/icon";

@Component({
  standalone: true,
  imports: [MatCardModule, MatButtonModule, MatIconModule],
  // ...
})
```

Common Material modules:

- `MatButtonModule` - Buttons
- `MatCardModule` - Cards
- `MatIconModule` - Icons
- `MatFormFieldModule` - Form fields
- `MatInputModule` - Inputs
- `MatDialogModule` - Dialogs
- `MatTableModule` - Tables
- `MatSnackBarModule` - Notifications

## 🏗️ Project Structure

```
MicroservicesArchitecture-Web/
├── apps/                    # All applications (50+ apps supported)
│   ├── web-app/            # Main application
│   │   ├── project.json    # Project configuration
│   │   └── src/
│   │       ├── app/        # Application code
│   │       └── styles/     # App-specific styles
│   └── playground/         # Playground application
│       ├── project.json    # Project configuration
│       └── src/
│           ├── app/        # Application code
│           └── styles/     # App-specific styles
├── libs/                   # Shared libraries
│   ├── core/              # Core services, guards, models
│   │   └── src/lib/
│   │       ├── models/    # Interfaces + Classes
│   │       ├── services/  # Injectable services
│   │       ├── guards/    # Route guards
│   │       └── interceptors/  # HTTP interceptors
│   └── shared/            # Shared components, pipes
│       └── src/lib/
│           ├── components/  # Reusable components
│           ├── pipes/       # Custom pipes
│           ├── directives/  # Custom directives
│           └── utils/       # Utility functions
└── e2e/                   # End-to-end tests
```

### Application Structure (Inside apps/\*)

```
apps/my-app/src/app/
├── features/              # Feature modules (lazy loaded)
│   ├── auth/
│   ├── dashboard/
│   └── [feature-name]/
└── layout/               # Layout components
```

Create folders **only when needed**. Start minimal.

## ✅ Best Practices Checklist

- ✅ Write MINIMAL code
- ✅ Use Angular Material components
- ✅ **Use Reactive Forms (FormGroup, FormControl)** - NEVER use Template-driven forms
- ✅ Private variables: `_variable`
- ✅ Observables: `variable$`
- ✅ Interface + Class for entities
- ✅ Signal `input()` and `output()` only
- ✅ CSS variables for theming
- ✅ SCSS: nest from `:host`
- ✅ Use standalone components
- ✅ Use `inject()` function
- ✅ Support dark/light mode
- ✅ Support RTL/LTR
- ✅ Delete unused imports/code
- ✅ OnPush change detection

## ❌ What NOT to Do

- ❌ Don't use `@Input()` / `@Output()`
- ❌ **Don't use Template-driven forms** - ALWAYS use Reactive Forms
- ❌ Don't create wrapper divs
- ❌ Don't hardcode colors
- ❌ Don't use flat SCSS
- ❌ Don't skip Interface + Class pattern
- ❌ Don't name private vars without `_`
- ❌ Don't name observables without `$`
- ❌ Don't use NgModules
- ❌ Don't use `any` type
- ❌ Don't import entire Material library
- ❌ Don't over-engineer

## 📋 Code Minimalism Rules

1. No unused imports - remove immediately
2. No unused variables/methods - delete them
3. No "just in case" code
4. No premature optimization
5. No wrapper divs - style `:host`
6. Use Material instead of custom components
7. Import only needed Material modules
8. Inline simple templates (< 5 lines)
9. Keep functions under 20 lines
10. Keep components under 200 lines

## 🔧 Component Template Example

```typescript
import { Component, input, output, signal } from '@angular/core';
import { MatCardModule } from '@angular/material/card';
import { MatButtonModule } from '@angular/material/button';

@Component({
  selector: 'app-user-card',
  standalone: true,
  imports: [MatCardModule, MatButtonModule],
  template: `
    <mat-card>
      <mat-card-header>
        <mat-card-title>{{ user().name }}</mat-card-title>
      </mat-card-header>
      @if (showActions()) {
      <mat-card-actions>
        <button mat-button (click)="onView()">View</button>
      </mat-card-actions>
      }
    </mat-card>
  `,
  styleUrl: './user-card.component.scss',
})
export class UserCardComponent {
  // Inputs
  user = input.required<User>();
  showActions = input<boolean>(true);

  // Outputs
  userClicked = output<User>();

  // Private state
  private _isLoading = signal<boolean>(false);

  onView() {
    this.userClicked.emit(this.user());
  }
}
```

## 🎨 SCSS Example

```scss
:host {
  display: block;

  mat-card {
    background-color: var(--background-color);
    border: 1px solid var(--border-color);
    transition: all 0.2s;

    &:hover {
      transform: translateY(-2px);
      box-shadow: var(--shadow);
    }

    mat-card-title {
      color: var(--text-color);
      font-size: var(--font-size-lg);
    }

    mat-card-actions {
      padding: var(--spacing-md);
      gap: var(--spacing-sm);
    }
  }

  // RTL Support
  :host-context([dir='rtl']) & {
    mat-card-actions {
      flex-direction: row-reverse;
    }
  }
}
```
